title: The photo or the morning
summary: Two friends in a Berlin park disagree about whether to take a photo of a beautiful moment or simply watch it.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: Look at the light on the lake. It is so beautiful. Take a photo!

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: I am looking at it. I do not want to take a photo.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: Why not? In one minute the light will change. Then it is gone.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Yes. It will be gone. That is why I am watching now, not later on a screen.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: But a photo keeps it. You can look again next year.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Can I? When I take a photo, I look at the small screen. I move. I check. I miss the real light. I keep a picture, but I lose the morning.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: I do not lose it. I take the photo fast, then I watch.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Maybe you do. I am different. My eyes are slow. If I do two things, I do both badly.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: But next year you will forget this morning. I will have my photo.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: You will have the photo. I will have the memory. They are not the same thing. A photo shows the light. A memory holds the cold air and your voice and the bird over there.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: Hmm. So you keep nothing on paper.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: I keep it in me. It is true that I forget some. But the ones I really watch — those stay. I think watching hard is its own kind of camera.

body.speaker: Lena
body.text: That is a nice idea. But I will still take one photo, for both of us.

body.speaker: Tomas
body.text: Good. You keep the light. I will keep the morning.

annotations.meaningVi: trên giấy (ở đây: lưu lại thành vật)

grammarNotes.title: 'will' for the future
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will + động từ nguyên thể' để nói về tương lai hoặc một dự đoán. Trong bài: 'In one minute the light will change.' và 'I will have the memory.' Dạng phủ định là 'will not' (won't). 'Will' thường dùng khi quyết định ngay lúc nói hoặc dự đoán.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + base verb' to talk about the future or a prediction. From the dialogue: 'In one minute the light will change.' and 'I will have the memory.' The negative is 'will not' (won't). 'Will' often marks a prediction or an on-the-spot decision.

translationVi:
Lena: Nhìn ánh sáng trên mặt hồ kìa. Đẹp quá. Chụp ảnh đi!
Tomas: Tớ đang nhìn nó đây. Tớ không muốn chụp ảnh.
Lena: Sao lại không? Một phút nữa ánh sáng sẽ đổi. Rồi nó mất.
Tomas: Ừ. Nó sẽ mất. Chính vì vậy tớ ngắm bây giờ, chứ không phải sau này trên màn hình.
Lena: Nhưng tấm ảnh giữ được nó. Sang năm cậu vẫn xem lại được.
Tomas: Thật à? Khi tớ chụp ảnh, tớ nhìn cái màn hình nhỏ. Tớ di chuyển. Tớ kiểm tra. Tớ bỏ lỡ ánh sáng thật. Tớ giữ một tấm hình, nhưng tớ mất buổi sáng.
Lena: Tớ không mất nó. Tớ chụp nhanh, rồi tớ ngắm.
Tomas: Có thể cậu làm được. Tớ thì khác. Mắt tớ chậm. Nếu làm hai việc một lúc, tớ làm cả hai đều dở.
Lena: Nhưng sang năm cậu sẽ quên buổi sáng này. Tớ thì có tấm ảnh.
Tomas: Cậu sẽ có tấm ảnh. Tớ sẽ có ký ức. Hai thứ đó không giống nhau. Tấm ảnh cho thấy ánh sáng. Ký ức giữ cả không khí lạnh, giọng cậu, và con chim đằng kia.
Lena: Hừm. Vậy cậu chẳng giữ gì trên giấy cả.
Tomas: Tớ giữ nó trong tớ. Đúng là tớ quên một số. Nhưng những gì tớ thật sự ngắm — thứ đó ở lại. Tớ nghĩ ngắm kỹ cũng là một kiểu máy ảnh.
Lena: Ý hay đấy. Nhưng tớ vẫn sẽ chụp một tấm, cho cả hai đứa.
Tomas: Được. Cậu giữ ánh sáng. Tớ sẽ giữ buổi sáng.

questions.prompt: What does Lena want Tomas to do?
options: Take a photo of the light on the lake

questions.prompt: Why does Tomas not want to take a photo?
options: He wants to watch the real light now
questions.explanation: 'That is why I am watching now, not later on a screen.'

questions.prompt: According to Tomas, what does he lose when he takes a photo?
questions.explanation: 'I keep a picture, but I lose the morning.'

questions.prompt: What does Lena say she does?
options: Takes the photo fast, then watches
questions.explanation: 'I take the photo fast, then I watch.'

questions.prompt: What does Tomas say a memory holds that a photo does not?
options: The cold air, her voice, and the bird
questions.explanation: 'A memory holds the cold air and your voice and the bird over there.'
questions.hint: His comparison of photo and memory.

questions.prompt: What does Tomas call 'its own kind of camera'?
questions.explanation: 'I think watching hard is its own kind of camera.'
questions.hint: Near the end of his lines.

questions.prompt: Does Tomas say he forgets some moments?
options: Yes, but the ones he really watches stay
questions.explanation: 'It is true that I forget some. But the ones I really watch — those stay.'

questions.prompt: What do they finally agree to do?
options: Lena keeps the light (photo), Tomas keeps the morning (memory)
questions.explanation: 'You keep the light. I will keep the morning.'

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the ending?
options: Friendly — they keep different views without a fight
questions.explanation: Both accept the other's way: 'Good. You keep the light.'

options: A recording and a lived memory keep different things; neither is wrong.
Photos are always better than memory.
Berlin lakes are beautiful in winter.
You should never use a phone.
questions.explanation: Each friend holds a real, different kind of keeping.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Tomas says watching hard is its own kind of camera. Think of the last time you photographed something. Did you watch it more, or less, because of the photo? Next time, try watching for one full minute before you decide.
